claim: Every domain MUST answer the overview question ("how is this domain structured as a whole") with ONE generated projection (OVERVIEW/PIPELINE.md), assembled from Process nodes plus EntityType plus the pipeline references between entities. This projection ALWAYS speaks in the language of the methodology/business-domain author: stage names are taken verbatim from Process.steps as authored, and any Go/code identifier appears ONLY as an adjacent annotation, never as a replacement for the author's own name. The projection's regenerability is the mechanical completeness test against the domain's original prose description: a stage or concept present in the source prose that does not surface anywhere in the projection is a discoverable coverage defect, not a silent gap.
why: Resolver finding this session (dogfooded on hotam-spec-self itself): the current substrate (graph + gen-spec docs) is memory FOR CHANGE — confront answers 'what does this contradict,' trace/HISTORY.md answers 'why was this rejected before,' what-now/UNENFORCED.md answers 'what is in debt now.' It is NOT memory FOR UNDERSTANDING — an overview answering 'how is this whole methodology/domain structured, stage by stage.' Verified concretely: an agent asked 'what are the stages of the PRAT methodology' could not answer better from the graph + generated docs than from reading the original prose document directly — no generated projection anywhere assembles Process.steps + EntityType + pipeline edges into one whole-structure view. Diagnosis has two halves. (a) The ontology already carries a §Process type for exactly this (Lifecycle + Steps + roles_required + drives_entities, internal/ontology/process.go) and hotam-spec-self itself uses it (PR-closed-loop, the ORIENT-LOCATE-CONFRONT-TRANSLATE-PRESENT-LAND process instantiated as data) -- but the `prat` business domain (PRAT-hotam/domains/prat) has declared zero Process nodes, so its own staged methodology (e.g. the OTT/GPSM stage sequence) has no typed anchor at all, only prose. (b) `hotam gen-spec` only emits confrontation-shaped docs (REQUIREMENTS.md, HISTORY.md, UNENFORCED.md, TENSIONS.md, ...) -- there is no generated projection of the whole across Process + EntityType + pipeline links. This requirement records the target state; building the generator (OVERVIEW/PIPELINE.md, assembled from Process nodes + EntityType + pipeline references) is tracked as follow-on task #201 and is deliberately NOT implemented by this requirement -- correctly PROSE/feature-blocked (R-speculative-aspects-frozen's sibling pattern: the claim is real and SETTLED, the enforcer does not exist yet, so it is honest roadmap debt, not a silent omission). Regenerability-as-completeness-test mirrors the same principle already SETTLED for gen-code's requirement-atom coverage audit (GEN-CODE-CONTRACT.md §3.1, resolver decision 2026-07-15): 'atom found' is not 'claim fully covered' there; here, 'a doc got generated' is not 'the whole structure is represented' -- both demand a mechanical diff against the original source (claim text there, prose document here), not a presence check. The language-fidelity clause (stage names verbatim from Process.steps, Go identifiers as annotation only) is the same discipline already SETTLED for R-crystal-carries-short-form and the zero-Cyrillic-in-code / audit-carries-original-text split in GEN-CODE-CONTRACT.md §1.1: the business author's own words are the primary artifact, code-facing names ride alongside, never replace them.
evidence: internal/ontology/process.go defines Process/Step/Lifecycle but the `prat` business domain (PRAT-hotam/domains/prat) declares zero Process nodes for its own staged methodology

hotam gen-spec output (domains/hotam-spec-self/docs/gen/*.md) contains no projection assembling Process.steps + EntityType + pipeline references into a single whole-structure overview — confirmed by directory listing (REQUIREMENTS.md, HISTORY.md, UNENFORCED.md, TENSIONS.md, CONSTITUTION.md, FRAMEWORK-INVARIANTS.md, GLOSSARY.md, REPO-MAP.md, live-state.md, AGENT-CONTEXT.md — all confrontation/status-shaped, none overview-shaped)
